user_request: Draft a suit for damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. Reproduce the defamatory statements in the body of the plaint. Plead malice, publication to third parties, loss of goodwill, and mental agony. Claim Rs.10,00,000/- as damages. Include prayer for permanent injunction restraining further publication on Facebook or any other social media platform. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court. Plead limitation correctly for defamation suits. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks damages for defamation against a Defendant for publishing false and malicious allegations on Facebook, which damaged the Plaintiff's business reputation. The Plaintiff claims Rs. 10,00,000 in damages, loss of goodwill, and mental agony, and seeks a permanent injunction against further publication.

plaint if by reason of their inclusion, the suit would be beyond the pecuniary jurisdiction of the court. 11 Where 
different suits which are severally within the jurisdiction of the court are consolidated for hearing, the court does 
not lose its jurisdiction to hear it by reason of the value of all the suits exceeding its jurisdiction if taken 
together.12
The Bombay High Court has held that in a case of damages, the amount of damage which the Court can award 
is limited to the pecuniary jurisdiction of the Court.13

A sues B in the court of second class subordinate judge for an account and values his suit for a court fee at Rs 
130. Under s 8 of the Suits Valuation Act, 1887, the value of the subject matter of the suit is Rs 130. The parties 
arrive at a compromise whereby the first defendant is to pay Rs 6,000 and the second defendant Rs 5,000 to 
the plaintiff. The court may pass a decree in terms of the compromise, although it has jurisdiction limited to suits 
of which the value of the subject matter does not exceed Rs 5,000.14
The forum of appeal also is determined by the value of the suit and not by the amount decreed.15

draft_artifacts.title: Suit for Damages for Defamation (Libel) and Permanent Injunction
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT JUDGE AT {{PLACE_OF_FILING}}

SUIT NO. ___ OF {{YEAR}}

IN THE MATTER OF:
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}}, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}                                      ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, {{DEFENDANT_AGE}}, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}                                       ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION (LIBEL) AND PERMANENT INJUNCTION

RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

1. That the Plaintiff is a {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}, and is a person of good reputation, standing and goodwill in the business community and society at {{PLACE_OF_FILING}}.

2. That the Defendant is {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years of age, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, and is acquainted with the Plaintiff.

3. That the Plaintiff has built substantial goodwill and reputation in his/her business/profession over the years and enjoys the confidence of business associates, customers and the general public.

4. That on or about {{DATE_OF_PUBLICATION}}, the Defendant maliciously and willfully published, posted and/or circulated the following false, baseless, scandalous and defamatory statements concerning the Plaintiff on the social media platform Facebook:

"{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_CONTENT}}"

(Hereinafter referred to as the "defamatory publication")

5. That the said defamatory statements were published by the Defendant with full knowledge of their falsity and with the deliberate intent to injure the Plaintiff's reputation and business, or with reckless disregard for the truth, thereby constituting express and implied malice.

6. That the defamatory publication was made to various third parties, including but not limited to, friends, business associates, customers, clients, and the general public who have access to the Defendant's Facebook profile and/or the said post, thereby seriously lowering the Plaintiff in the estimation of right-thinking members of society and causing grave injury to the Plaintiff's reputation and business standing.

7. That as a direct and proximate result of the said defamatory publication, the Plaintiff has suffered:
(i) Loss of goodwill, reputation and business standing;
(ii) Mental agony, pain, suffering and injury to dignity and self-respect;
(iii) Loss of business opportunities and income;
(iv) Humiliation and embarrassment in the eyes of the public and business community.

8. That the Plaintiff has quantified the damages suffered due to the aforesaid defamation at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only), for which amount the Plaintiff is entitled to recover from the Defendant.

9. That the defamatory post referred to in paragraph 4 above continues to remain accessible and visible on Facebook and/or other social media platforms under the control of the Defendant, causing continuing and ongoing injury to the Plaintiff's reputation and goodwill.

CAUSE OF ACTION:
10. That the cause of action arose on {{DATE_OF_PUBLICATION}}, when the Defendant first published the defamatory statements on Facebook, thereby causing injury to the Plaintiff's reputation and business. The right to sue accrued to the Plaintiff on the said date under the applicable law of limitation. The cause of action is of a continuing nature insofar as the defamatory content remains published and accessible to the public, causing continuing injury to the Plaintiff's reputation and business interests, save and except that the primary cause of action accrued on the date of first publication.

LIMITATION:
11. That the present suit is filed well within the period of limitation prescribed under {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides for a limitation period of {{LIMITATION_PERIOD}} from the date of publication of the libel. The suit is not barred by limitation under Section 3 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

JURISDICTION:
12. That this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to try and entertain this suit as the cause of action arose wholly or in part within the territorial jurisdiction of this Court at {{PLACE_OF_FILING}}, and/or the Defendant resides or carries on business within the jurisdiction of this Court, as per the provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE:
13. That the suit is valued at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only) for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee. The requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} has been paid on the plaint as per the Court Fees Act, 1870.

PRAYER:
In view of the facts and circumstances stated above, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

(A) Pass a decree awarding damages of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only) in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for the defamation suffered by the Plaintiff;

(B) Grant a permanent injunction restraining the Defendant, his agents, servants, assigns, or anyone acting on his behalf from publishing, republishing, circulating, communicating, or causing to be published any defamatory statements concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook, WhatsApp, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn, or any other social media platform, website, or public forum;

(C) Direct the Defendant to forthwith delete, remove and take down the defamatory content referred to in paragraph 4 above from Facebook and all other platforms under his control or access;

(D) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;

(E) Pass such other order(s) and grant such further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

Place: {{PLACE_OF_FILING}}
Date: {{DATE}}

VERIFICATION:
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, son/daughter of {{FATHER_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}}, occupation {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}, do hereby verify and state on solemn affirmation as follows:

1. That the contents of paragraphs 1 to 9 and 13 of the plaint are true to my personal knowledge;
2. That the contents of paragraphs 10, 11, and 12 are based on legal advice and believed to be true;
3. That I have not concealed any material fact.

Signed this {{DATE}} at {{PLACE_OF_FILING}}

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
... Plaintiff

LIST OF DOCUMENTS:
1. Screenshot/Printout of the defamatory Facebook post dated {{DATE_OF_PUBLICATION}} marked as Annexure-A
2. Proof of reputation/standing of the Plaintiff (certificates/awards if any) marked as Annexure-B
3. Any correspondence/notice sent to Defendant regarding removal of content marked as Annexure-C
4. Affidavit in support of injunction application marked as Annexure-D

draft_artifacts.title: Suit for Damages for Defamation (Libel) and Permanent Injunction
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT JUDGE AT {{PLACE_OF_FILING}}

SUIT NO. ___ OF {{YEAR}}

IN THE MATTER OF:
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}}, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}                                      ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, {{DEFENDANT_AGE}}, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}                                       ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION (LIBEL) AND PERMANENT INJUNCTION

RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

1. That the Plaintiff is a {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}, and is a person of good reputation, standing and goodwill in the business community and society at {{PLACE_OF_FILING}}, as evidenced by certificates and awards annexed hereto and marked as Annexure-B.

2. That the Defendant is {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years of age, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, and is acquainted with the Plaintiff.

3. That the Plaintiff has built substantial goodwill and reputation in his/her business/profession over the years and enjoys the confidence of business associates, customers and the general public.

4. That on or about {{DATE_OF_PUBLICATION}}, the Defendant maliciously and willfully published, posted and/or circulated the following false, baseless, scandalous and defamatory statements concerning the Plaintiff on the social media platform Facebook:

"{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_CONTENT}}"

(Hereinafter referred to as the "defamatory publication"), as per the screenshot/printout annexed hereto and marked as Annexure-A.

5. That the said defamatory statements were published by the Defendant with full knowledge of their falsity and with the deliberate intent to injure the Plaintiff's reputation and business, or with reckless disregard for the truth, thereby constituting express and implied malice.

6. That the defamatory publication was made to various third parties, including but not limited to, friends, business associates, customers, clients, and the general public who have access to the Defendant's Facebook profile and/or the said post, thereby seriously lowering the Plaintiff in the estimation of right-thinking members of society and causing grave injury to the Plaintiff's reputation and business standing.

7. That as a direct and proximate result of the said defamatory publication, the Plaintiff has suffered:
(i) Loss of goodwill, reputation and business standing;
(ii) Mental agony, pain, suffering and injury to dignity and self-respect;
(iii) Loss of business opportunities and income;
(iv) Humiliation and embarrassment in the eyes of the public and business community.

8. That the Plaintiff has quantified the damages suffered due to the aforesaid defamation at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only), for which amount the Plaintiff is entitled to recover from the Defendant. Prior to the institution of the present suit, the Plaintiff, through his Advocate, caused a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} to be served upon the Defendant calling upon him to forthwith remove the defamatory content and tender an unconditional public apology, which notice was duly received by the Defendant but the Defendant has failed and neglected to comply with the lawful demands therein. A copy of the said legal notice along with proof of service is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure-C.

9. That the defamatory post referred to in paragraph 4 above continues to remain accessible and visible on Facebook and/or other social media platforms under the control of the Defendant, causing continuing and ongoing injury to the Plaintiff's reputation and goodwill. The Plaintiff has filed an affidavit in support of the prayer for permanent injunction, a copy whereof is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure-D.

CAUSE OF ACTION:
10. That the cause of action arose on {{DATE_OF_PUBLICATION}}, when the Defendant first published the defamatory statements on Facebook, thereby causing injury to the Plaintiff's reputation and business. The right to sue accrued to the Plaintiff on the said date under the applicable law of limitation. The cause of action is of a continuing nature insofar as the defamatory content remains published and accessible to the public, causing continuing injury to the Plaintiff's reputation and business interests, save and except that the primary cause of action accrued on the date of first publication.

LIMITATION:
11. That the present suit is filed well within the period of limitation prescribed under {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides for a limitation period of {{LIMITATION_PERIOD}} from the date of publication of the libel. The suit is not barred by limitation under Section 3 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

JURISDICTION:
12. That this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to try and entertain this suit as the cause of action arose wholly or in part within the territorial jurisdiction of this Court at {{PLACE_OF_FILING}}, and/or the Defendant resides or carries on business within the jurisdiction of this Court, as per the provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE:
13. That the suit is valued at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only) for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee. The requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} has been paid on the plaint as per the Court Fees Act, 1870.

PRAYER:
In view of the facts and circumstances stated above, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

(A) Pass a decree awarding damages of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only) in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for the defamation suffered by the Plaintiff;

(B) Grant a permanent injunction restraining the Defendant, his agents, servants, assigns, or anyone acting on his behalf from publishing, republishing, circulating, communicating, or causing to be published any defamatory statements concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook, WhatsApp, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn, or any other social media platform, website, or public forum;

(C) Direct the Defendant to forthwith delete, remove and take down the defamatory content referred to in paragraph 4 above from Facebook and all other platforms under his control or access;

(D) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;

(E) Pass such other order(s) and grant such further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

Place: {{PLACE_OF_FILING}}
Date: {{DATE}}

VERIFICATION:
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, son/daughter of {{FATHER_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}}, occupation {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}, do hereby verify and state on solemn affirmation as follows:

1. That the contents of paragraphs 1 to 9 and 13 of the plaint are true to my personal knowledge;
2. That the contents of paragraphs 10, 11, and 12 are based on legal advice and believed to be true;
3. That I have not concealed any material fact.

Signed this {{DATE}} at {{PLACE_OF_FILING}}

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
... Plaintiff

LIST OF DOCUMENTS:
1. Screenshot/Printout of the defamatory Facebook post dated {{DATE_OF_PUBLICATION}} marked as Annexure-A
2. Proof of reputation/standing of the Plaintiff (certificates/awards if any) marked as Annexure-B
3. Any correspondence/notice sent to Defendant regarding removal of content marked as Annexure-C
4. Affidavit in support of injunction application marked as Annexure-D
